task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Validated Registration Form** (Level 7)

Build a registration endpoint POST /api/register with strict validation: email (required, valid email format), password (required, min 8 chars), name (required, max 100 chars), age (number, min 13). Return detailed error messages on 400.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required, min 8
  name, required, max 100
  age, number

# Your end
final_source:
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required, min 8
  name, required, max 100
  age, number, min 13

when user sends data to /api/register:
  rate limit 10 per minute
  
  if data's email is empty:
    send back {'error': 'email is required'} with status 400
  
  email_str = data's email
  has_at_sign = '@' in email_str
  if has_at_sign is false:
    send back {'error': 'email must be a valid email format'} with status 400
  
  if data's password is empty:
    send back {'error': 'password is required'} with status 400
  
  pass_len = length of data's password
  if pass_len is less than 8:
    send back {'error': 'password must have at least 8 characters'} with status 400
  
  if data's name is empty:
    send back {'error': 'name is required'} with status 400
  
  name_len = length of data's name
  if name_len is greater than 100:
    send back {'error': 'name must be at most 100 characters'} with status 400
  
  if data's age is not empty:
    if data's age is less than 13:
      send back {'error': 'age must be at least 13'} with status 400
  
  saved = save data to Users
  send back saved with status 201

test 'valid registration':
  call POST /api/register with {'email': 'valid@test.com', 'password': 'longpassword', 'name': 'Alice', 'age': 25}
  expect response status 201
  expect response has id

test 'invalid email':
  call POST /api/register with {'email': 'bad', 'password': 'longpassword', 'name': 'Bob'}
  expect response status 400

test 'short password':
  call POST /api/register with {'email': 'x@test.com', 'password': 'short', 'name': 'Carol'}
  expect response status 400

test 'empty input':
  call POST /api/register with {}
  expect response status 400
